section_name: Type of Vehicle Required to Be Indicated on License
text:
(a) The department shall show on each driver’s license the general type of vehicle that the license holder is authorized to operate.

(b) The department may include on the driver’s license an authorization to operate a motorcycle if the license holder has met all requirements for a Class M license.

Sec. 521.123. DESIGNATOR ON LICENSE ISSUED TO PERSON UNDER 21 YEARS OF AGE. The department shall:

(1) designate and clearly mark as a provisional license each original driver’s license issued by the department to a person who is under 18 years of age; and

(2) for each original, renewed, or duplicate license issued to a person who is under 21 years of age:

(A) indicate “UNDER 21” on the face of the license; and

(B) orient the information on the license to clearly distinguish the license from a license that is issued to a person who is 21 years of age or older.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.122 — Type of Vehicle Required to Be Indicated on License

(a) The department shall show on each driver’s license the general type of vehicle that the license holder is authorized to operate.

(b) The department may include on the driver’s license an authorization to operate a motorcycle if the license holder has met all requirements for a Class M license.

Sec. 521.123. DESIGNATOR ON LICENSE ISSUED TO PERSON UNDER 21 YEARS OF AGE. The department shall:

    (1) designate and clearly mark as a provisional license each original driver’s license issued by the department to a person who is under 18 years of age; and

    (2) for each original, renewed, or duplicate license issued to a person who is under 21 years of age:

        (A) indicate “UNDER 21” on the face of the license; and

        (B) orient the information on the license to clearly distinguish the license from a license that is issued to a person who is 21 years of age or older.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 448, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The department shall show on each driver’s license the general type of vehicle that the license holder is authorized to operate.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The department may include on the driver’s license an authorization to operate a motorcycle if the license holder has met all requirements for a Class M license.
Sec. 521.123. DESIGNATOR ON LICENSE ISSUED TO PERSON UNDER 21 YEARS OF AGE. The department shall:
designate and clearly mark as a provisional license each original driver’s license issued by the department to a person who is under 18 years of age; and
for each original, renewed, or duplicate license issued to a person who is under 21 years of age:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: indicate “UNDER 21” on the face of the license; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: orient the information on the license to clearly distinguish the license from a license that is issued to a person who is 21 years of age or older.
